rationale: The cited paper is highly aligned with the citing paper’s core constructs, as both focus on variety-seeking and nonconscious visual priming effects on consumer choice. The citing paper’s design note includes a visual priming task to activate variety-seeking versus inertia, which plausibly draws on the cited paper’s shape-array priming paradigm and prior evidence that such primes shift choice behavior. Given this potential theory/method foundation (and only DOI-level match evidence), this pair should be retained for full relation judgment rather than dropped as incidental.
